functions.name: rospkg.environment._resolve_path
documentation: Utilities

functions.name: rospkg.environment._resolve_paths
documentation:
Catch-all utility routine for fixing ROS environment variables
that are paths (e.g. ROS_PACKAGE_PATH).  Currently this just
expands tildes to home directories, but in the future it may
encode other behaviors.

```
param paths

path string with OS-defined separator (i.e. ':' for Linux), str
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_ros_paths
documentation:
Get an ordered list of ROS paths to search for ROS packages,
stacks, and other resources.  This is generally computed from
{envvar}`ROS_ROOT` and {envvar}`ROS_PACKAGE_PATH`.

```
param env

override environment dictionary
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_ros_root
documentation:
Get the current {envvar}`ROS_ROOT`.

```
param env

override environment dictionary, dict
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_ros_package_path
documentation:
Get the current ROS_PACKAGE_PATH.
\:param env: (optional) environment override, `dict`

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_ros_home
documentation:
Get directory location of '.ros' directory (aka ROS home).
possible locations for this. The {envvar}`ROS_HOME` environment
variable has priority. If {envvar}`ROS_HOME` is not set,
`$HOME/.ros` is used.

```
param env

override os.environ dictionary, dict

returns

path to use use for log file directory, str
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_log_dir
documentation:
Get directory to use for writing log files. There are multiple
possible locations for this. The ROS_LOG_DIR environment variable
has priority. If that is not set, then ROS_HOME/log is used. If
ROS_HOME is not set, \$HOME/.ros/log is used.

```
param env

override os.environ dictionary, dict

returns

path to use use for log file directory, str
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_test_results_dir
documentation:
Get directory to use for writing test result files. There are
multiple possible locations for this. The
{envvar}`ROS_TEST_RESULTS_DIR` environment variable has
priority. If that is set, {envvar}`ROS_TEST_RESULTS_DIR` is
returned.  If {envvar}`ROS_TEST_RESULTS_DIR` is not set, then
{envvar}`ROS_HOME`/test_results is used. If {envvar}`ROS_HOME` is
not set, `$HOME/.ros/test_results` is used.

```
param env

environment dictionary (defaults to os.environ), dict

returns

path to use use for log file directory, str
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment._compute_package_paths
documentation:
Get the paths to search for packages in normal precedence order (i.e. first path wins).

```
param ros_root

value of ROS_ROOT parameter, str

param ros_package_path

value of ROS_PACKAGE_PATH parameter, str

returns

paths to search in reverse order of precedence, [str]
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.on_ros_path
documentation:
Check to see if filesystem path is on paths specified in ROS
environment ({envvar}`ROS_ROOT`, {envvar}`ROS_PACKAGE_PATH`).

```
param p

path, str

return

True if p is on the ROS path, bool
```

functions.name: rospkg.environment.get_etc_ros_dir
documentation:
Get directory location of `/etc/ros` directory.  The
{envvar}`ROS_ETC_DIR` environment variable has priority. If
{envvar}`ROS_ETC_DIR` is not set, `/etc/ros` is used.

```
param env

override environment dictionary

return

path to use use for log file directory
```

functions.name: rospkg.rospack.list_by_path
documentation:
List ROS stacks or packages within the specified path.

The cache will be updated with the resource->path
mappings. list_by_path() does NOT returned cached results
-- it only updates the cache.

```
param manifest_name

MANIFEST_FILE or STACK_FILE, str

param path

path to list resources in, str

param cache

path cache to update. Maps resource name to directory path, {str: str}

returns

complete list of resources in ROS environment, [str]
```

functions.name: rospkg.rospack.expand_to_packages
documentation:
Expand names into a list of packages. Names can either be of packages or stacks.

```
param names

names of stacks or packages, [str]

returns

([packages], [not_found]). Returns two lists. The first
is of packages names. The second is a list of names for which no
matching stack or package was found. Lists may have
duplicates. ([str], [str])
```

functions.name: rospkg.rospack.get_stack_version_by_dir
documentation:
Get stack version where stack_dir points to root directory of stack.

```
param env

override environment variables, {str: str}

returns

version number of stack, or None if stack is unversioned, str

raises



raises

```

functions.name: rospkg.rospack._get_cmake_version
documentation: \:raises {exc}`ValueError` If version number in CMakeLists.txt cannot be parsed correctly

functions.name: rospkg.rospack.get_package_name
documentation:
Get the name of the ROS package that contains *path*. This is
determined by finding the nearest parent `manifest.xml` file.
This routine may not traverse package setups that rely on internal
symlinks within the package itself.

```
param path

filesystem path

return

Package name or None if package cannot be found, str
```

ctor. subclasses are expected to use *manifest_name*

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.ManifestManager.get_depends
documentation:
Get dependencies of a resource.  If implicit is `True`, this
includes implicit (recursive) dependencies.

```
param name

resource name, str

param implicit

include implicit (recursive) dependencies, bool

returns

list of names of dependencies, [str]

raises

 If resource or any of its
dependencies have an invalid manifest.
```

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.ManifestManager.get_depends_on
documentation:
Get resources that depend on a resource.  If implicit is `True`, this
includes implicit (recursive) dependency relationships.

NOTE: this does *not* raise {exc}`rospkg.InvalidManifest` if
there are invalid manifests found.

```
param name

resource name, str

param implicit

include implicit (recursive) dependencies, bool

returns

list of names of dependencies, [str]
```

Base class implementation for {class}`RosPack` and
{class}`RosStack`.  This class indexes resources on paths with
where manifests denote the precense of the resource.  NOTE: for
performance reasons, instances cache information and will not
reflect changes made on disk or to environment configuration.

classes.name: rospkg.rospack.RosPack

documentation:
---
param ros_paths: |-
  Ordered list of paths to search for
  resources. If `None` (default), use environment ROS path.
---

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.RosPack.get_rosdeps
documentation:
Collect rosdeps of specified package into a dictionary.

```
param package

package name, str

param implicit

include implicit (recursive) rosdeps, bool

returns

list of rosdep names, [str]

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.RosPack._implicit_rosdeps
documentation:
Compute recursive rosdeps of a single package and cache the
result in self.\_rosdeps_cache.

```
param package

package name, str

returns

list of rosdeps, [str]
```

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.RosPack.stack_of
documentation:
---
param package: package name, `str`
raises: '{exc}`ResourceNotFound` If package cannot be located'
returns: name of stack that package is in, or None if package is not part of a stack,
  `str`
---

Utility class for querying properties about ROS packages. This
should be used when querying properties about multiple
packages.

NOTE 1: for performance reasons, RosPack caches information about
packages.

NOTE 2: RosPack is not thread-safe.

Example::

: from rospkg import RosPack
  rp = RosPack()
  packages = rp.list()
  path = rp.get_path('rospy')
  depends = rp.get_depends('roscpp')
  direct_depends = rp.get_depends('roscpp', implicit=False)

classes.name: rospkg.rospack.RosStack

documentation:
---
param ros_paths: |-
  Ordered list of paths to search for
  resources. If `None` (default), use environment ROS path.
---

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.RosStack.packages_of
documentation:
---
raises: '{exc}`ResourceNotFound` If stack cannot be located'
returns: name of packages that are part of stack, `[str]`
---

methods.name: rospkg.rospack.RosStack.get_stack_version
documentation:
---
param env: 'override environment variables, `{str: str}`'
returns: version number of stack, or None if stack is unversioned, `str`
---

Utility class for querying properties about ROS stacks. This
should be used when querying properties about multiple
stacks.

NOTE 1: for performance reasons, RosStack caches information about
stacks.

NOTE 2: RosStack is not thread-safe.

functions.name: rospkg.distro.distro_uri
documentation:
Get distro URI of main ROS distribution files.

```
param distro_name

name of distro, e.g. 'diamondback'

returns

the SVN/HTTP URL of the specified distro.  This function should only be used
with the main distros.
```

functions.name: rospkg.distro.load_distro
documentation:
---
param source_uri: |-
  source URI of distro file, or path to distro
  file.  Filename has precedence in resolution.
raises: '{exc}`ResourceNotFound` If file at *source_uri* is not found'
---

functions.name: rospkg.distro._load_distro_stacks
documentation:
---
param distro_doc: dictionary form of rosdistro file, `dict`
raises: '{exc}`InvalidDistro` if distro_doc format is invalid'
returns: 'dictionary of stack names to {class}`DistroStack` instances, `{str : DistroStack}`'
---

functions.name: rospkg.distro._distro_version
documentation: Parse distro version value, converting SVN revision to version value if necessary

functions.name: rospkg.distro.distro_to_rosinstall
documentation:
---
param anonymous: create for anonymous access rules
param branch: branch to convert for
param implicit: if variant_name is provided, include full (recursive) dependencies
  of variant, default True
param released_only: only included released stacks, default True.
param variant_name: if not None, only include stacks in the specified variant.
raises: '{exc}`KeyError` If branch is invalid or if distro is mis-configured'
returns: rosinstall data in Python list format, `[dict]`
---

Retrieve rules from distro_doc for specified stack.  This operates on
the raw distro dictionary document.

rosdistro document, dict

param stack_name

name of stack to get rules for, str
```

  Dictionary of supported {class}`VcsConfig` instances.

rosdistro rules data

Function to apply to rule values, e.g. to
convert variables.  fn(str)->str

returns

 subclass instance with interpreted rules data.

param raw_data: raw rosdistro data for this variant
param stack_names_implicit: full list of stacks implicitly included in this variant,
  `[str]`
param variant_name: name of variant to load from distro file, `str`
---

A variant defines a specific set of stacks ("metapackage", in Debian
parlance). For example, "base", "pr2". These variants can extend
another variant.

Base representation of a rosdistro VCS rules configuration.

classes.name: rospkg.distro.DvcsConfig

documentation:
---
raises: '{exc}`KeyError` Invalid branch parameter'
---

Configuration information for a distributed VCS-style repository.

Configuration fields:

> - `repo_uri`: base URI of repo
> - `dev_branch`: git branch the code is developed
> - `distro_tag`: a tag of the latest released code for a specific ROS distribution
> - `release_tag`: a tag of the code for a specific release

classes.name: rospkg.distro.GitConfig
documentation: Configuration information about an GIT repository. See parent class {class}`DvcsConfig` for more API information.

classes.name: rospkg.distro.HgConfig
documentation: Configuration information about a Mercurial repository. See parent class {class}`DvcsConfig` for more API information.

classes.name: rospkg.distro.BzrConfig

documentation:
---
raises: '{exc}`ValueError` If branch is invalid'
---

Configuration information about an SVN repository.

Configuration fields:

> - `dev`: where the code is developed
> - `distro_tag`: a tag of the code for a specific ROS distribution
> - `release_tag`: a tag of the code for a specific release

Linux distributions.

functions.name: rospkg.os_detect.uname_get_machine
documentation: Linux: wrapper around uname to determine if OS is 64-bit

functions.name: rospkg.os_detect.read_issue
documentation: :returns: list of strings in issue file, or None if issue file cannot be read/split

functions.name: rospkg.os_detect.read_os_release
documentation:
:returns: Dictionary of key value pairs from /etc/os-release or fallback to 
  /usr/lib/os-release, with quotes stripped from values

classes.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsNotDetected
documentation: Exception to indicate failure to detect operating system.

classes.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetector

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetector.is_os
documentation:
:returns: if the specific OS which this class is designed to
  detect is present.  Only one version of this class should
  return for any version.

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetector.get_version
documentation:
---
raises: '{exc}`OsNotDetected` if called on incorrect OS.'
returns: standardized version for this OS. (aka Ubuntu Hardy Heron = "8.04")
---

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetector.get_codename
documentation:
---
raises: '{exc}`OsNotDetected` if called on incorrect OS.'
returns: codename for this OS. (aka Ubuntu Hardy Heron = "hardy").  If codenames are
  not available for this OS, return empty string.
---
Generic API for detecting a specific OS.

classes.name: rospkg.os_detect.LsbDetect
documentation: Generic detector for Debian, Ubuntu, Mint, and Pop! OS

classes.name: rospkg.os_detect.FdoDetect
documentation:
Generic detector for operating systems implementing /etc/os-release, as defined by the os-release spec hosted at Freedesktop.org (Fdo):
http://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/os-release.html
Requires that the "ID", and "VERSION_ID" keys are set in the os-release file.

Codename is parsed from the VERSION key if available: either using the format "foo, CODENAME" or "foo (CODENAME)."
If the VERSION key is not present, the VERSION_ID is value is used as the codename.

documentation: Detect OpenEmbedded.

documentation: Detect Conda.

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetect.register_default
documentation:
Register detector to be used with all future instances of
{class}`OsDetect`.  The new detector will have precedence over
any previously registered detectors associated with *os_name*.

```
param os_name

OS key associated with OS detector

param os_detector

 instance
```

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetect.detect_os
documentation:
Detect operating system.  Return value can be overridden by
the {env}`ROS_OS_OVERRIDE` environment variable.

```
param env

override os.environ

returns

(os_name, os_version, os_codename), (str, str, str)

raises

 if OS could not be detected
```

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetect.get_detector
documentation:
Get detector used for specified OS name, or the detector for this OS if name is `None`.

```
raises

```

methods.name: rospkg.os_detect.OsDetect.add_detector
documentation:
Add detector to list of detectors used by this instance.  *detector* will override any previous
detectors associated with *name*.

```
param name

OS name that detector matches

param detector

 instance
```

This class will iterate over registered classes to lookup the
active OS and version
